qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in societe/ajaxcompanies.php filter parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL injection in the rowid parameter of admin/dict.php. This finding describes SQL injection in the filter parameter of societe/ajaxcompanies.php. These are different endpoints, different parameters, different SQL query contexts, and different injection sinks. While both are SQL injection vulnerabilities in Dolibarr, the root-cause code locations do not overlap: the advisory points to admin/dict.php while the finding points to societe/ajaxcompanies.php:84. Same broad vulnerability class is supporting context only.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The sole qualifying finding (sql_injection_ajaxcompanies) describes SQL injection in societe/ajaxcompanies.php via the filter parameter, which is a different endpoint, parameter, and injection sink from the advisory-described vulnerability in admin/dict.php via the rowid parameter.
evidence: Advisory specifies SQL injection in admin/dict.php via rowid parameter.
sql_injection_ajaxcompanies targets societe/ajaxcompanies.php via filter parameter: different file, different parameter, different injection sink.
